_meta.verificationNote: Sample cyber risk register with 20 risks mapped to NIST CSF 2.0 functions, categories, and subcategories. Risk ratings, controls, and treatment plans are indicative examples for organizational adaptation. Actual assessments must reflect organization-specific context.

title: Cyber Risk Register
description: 20 cyber risks aligned to NIST CSF 2.0 functions and subcategories, assessed using the 5x5 risk matrix methodology with inherent and residual risk ratings.

risks.title: Incomplete Asset Inventory
risks.description: Organization lacks a comprehensive, current inventory of hardware, software, data assets, and cloud services, leading to unmanaged and unprotected assets that expand the attack surface.
existingControls: Partial network discovery tool deployed
Manual spreadsheet-based inventory updated quarterly
New asset procurement tracked through IT ticketing
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy automated asset discovery and management platform; integrate with CMDB; establish continuous reconciliation process with 48-hour SLA for new asset registration.

risks.title: Unmanaged Software Vulnerabilities
risks.description: Known software vulnerabilities remain unpatched beyond acceptable timelines due to inconsistent patch management processes, leaving systems exposed to exploitation.
existingControls: Monthly patch cycle for critical servers
Vulnerability scanner runs weekly on internal network
Emergency patching process for critical CVEs
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement automated patch management for all endpoints; establish risk-based SLAs (critical: 72h, high: 7d, medium: 30d); deploy virtual patching for legacy systems that cannot be patched directly.

risks.title: Weak Identity Management
risks.description: Inconsistent identity lifecycle management including orphaned accounts, shared credentials, and lack of centralized identity governance creates unauthorized access risks.
existingControls: Active Directory for centralized authentication
Quarterly access review for privileged accounts
Password complexity policy enforced
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy identity governance and administration (IGA) platform; automate joiner-mover-leaver processes; implement continuous access certification for all accounts; integrate HR system with identity provisioning.

risks.title: Insufficient Access Controls
risks.description: Excessive privilege assignments, lack of least-privilege enforcement, and absence of multi-factor authentication on critical systems increase the risk of unauthorized access and lateral movement.
existingControls: Role-based access control on major applications
MFA deployed for VPN and email
Privileged access management tool for server access
risks.treatmentPlan: Extend MFA to all business-critical applications; implement just-in-time privileged access; deploy network microsegmentation; establish quarterly privilege audits with automated revocation.

risks.title: Inadequate Security Awareness Training
risks.description: Workforce lacks sufficient cybersecurity awareness, making the organization vulnerable to social engineering, phishing, and insider threats due to human error.
existingControls: Annual security awareness training for all staff
Quarterly phishing simulation campaigns
New hire security onboarding module
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement continuous microlearning platform with role-based content; increase phishing simulation frequency to monthly; establish department-level security champions program; track and report training completion and phish-prone rate KPIs.

risks.title: Unencrypted Sensitive Data
risks.description: Sensitive data (PII, financial records, intellectual property) is stored or transmitted without adequate encryption, exposing it to unauthorized disclosure in the event of breach or interception.
existingControls: TLS 1.2+ enforced for all web applications
Full disk encryption on company-managed laptops
Database encryption for production financial systems
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct data classification exercise to identify all sensitive data stores; implement encryption-at-rest for all databases and file shares containing classified data; deploy DLP tools to detect unencrypted sensitive data in transit; upgrade to TLS 1.3 where supported.

risks.title: Misconfigured Security Tools
risks.description: Security controls including firewalls, endpoint protection, and SIEM are deployed but misconfigured, resulting in blind spots, false negatives, and an inflated sense of security posture.
existingControls: Documented baseline configurations for firewalls
SIEM correlation rules reviewed semi-annually
Endpoint protection managed by central console
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement infrastructure-as-code for security tool configurations; establish monthly configuration audit against CIS benchmarks; deploy configuration drift detection; conduct quarterly red team exercises to validate control effectiveness.

risks.title: Insufficient Logging and Monitoring
risks.description: Critical systems and network segments lack adequate logging, and existing logs are not forwarded to centralized monitoring, preventing timely detection of security events.
existingControls: SIEM deployed with logs from core infrastructure
Network IDS on perimeter segments
Cloud audit logging enabled for primary cloud provider
risks.treatmentPlan: Audit all systems for log forwarding completeness; extend SIEM log sources to cover all critical assets per asset inventory; implement log integrity monitoring; establish minimum retention of 90 days hot and 1 year archive for security-relevant logs.

risks.title: Delayed Threat Detection
risks.description: Mean time to detect (MTTD) security incidents exceeds acceptable thresholds due to insufficient correlation rules, analyst capacity, and automation, allowing attackers extended dwell time.
existingControls: 24/5 SOC monitoring during business hours
Automated alerting for high-severity SIEM rules
Threat intelligence feed integrated with perimeter controls
risks.treatmentPlan: Transfer 24/7 detection and response capability to a managed detection and response (MDR) provider with guaranteed SLAs for MTTD (<1 hour critical, <4 hours high); retain internal SOC for triage oversight, threat hunting, and vendor management; include cyber insurance coverage for detection failures leading to breach.

risks.title: No Incident Response Plan
risks.description: Organization lacks a formally documented, tested, and regularly updated incident response plan, leading to ad-hoc and ineffective responses that increase incident impact and recovery time.
existingControls: Informal escalation procedures known to senior IT staff
Incident tracking via IT service management tool
External IR retainer contract with cybersecurity firm
risks.treatmentPlan: Develop formal incident response plan aligned to NIST SP 800-61; conduct tabletop exercises quarterly; establish incident severity classification matrix; document communication protocols for internal, legal, regulatory, and media stakeholders.

risks.title: Poor Incident Communication
risks.description: Lack of predefined communication protocols for cybersecurity incidents leads to delayed or inconsistent notifications to stakeholders, regulators, and affected parties.
existingControls: General corporate crisis communication plan exists
Legal counsel available for breach notification guidance
Executive escalation matrix documented
risks.treatmentPlan: Develop cyber-specific incident communication playbooks for each severity level; pre-draft notification templates for regulators, customers, and media; integrate communication steps into IR tabletop exercises; establish communication SLAs per stakeholder type.

risks.title: Inadequate Forensic Capability
risks.description: Organization lacks sufficient digital forensics tools, trained personnel, and evidence preservation procedures, compromising ability to determine root cause and support legal proceedings.
existingControls: External forensics retainer with 4-hour SLA
Endpoint detection and response tool with basic forensics
Network packet capture on critical segments
risks.treatmentPlan: Build internal basic forensics capability (2 trained analysts); deploy forensic imaging and analysis tools; document evidence chain-of-custody procedures; establish forensic readiness requirements for critical systems (log retention, memory dump capability).

risks.title: Untested Disaster Recovery
risks.description: Disaster recovery plans exist but are not regularly tested, leading to uncertain recovery capability, potentially extended outages, and data loss during actual incidents.
existingControls: DR plan documented for tier-1 applications
Offsite backups replicated daily
Annual DR test for core ERP system
risks.treatmentPlan: Expand DR testing to all tier-1 and tier-2 applications on quarterly cycle; conduct full failover test annually; establish and validate RTO/RPO targets per application tier; automate DR orchestration and failover procedures where feasible.

risks.title: No Business Continuity Plan
risks.description: Organization lacks a comprehensive business continuity plan that addresses sustained cyber incidents, leading to uncoordinated response and prolonged operational impact.
existingControls: Informal recovery procedures for critical systems
Key personnel contact list maintained
Cloud-based email ensures communication continuity
risks.treatmentPlan: Develop comprehensive BCP covering cyber incident scenarios; conduct business impact analysis to identify critical processes and dependencies; establish alternate processing procedures; test BCP through scenario-based exercises semi-annually.

risks.title: Governance Framework Gaps
risks.description: Cybersecurity governance structures, policies, and oversight mechanisms are incomplete or outdated, leaving unclear accountability and inconsistent risk management practices across the organization.
existingControls: CISO reports to CIO with quarterly board briefing
Information security policy published and reviewed annually
Security steering committee meets monthly
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct governance maturity assessment against NIST CSF 2.0 GV function; establish direct CISO reporting to CEO or board committee; formalize cybersecurity charter with defined authority and accountability; implement policy lifecycle management with annual review and version control.

risks.title: Risk Appetite Not Defined
risks.description: Organization has not formally defined and communicated its cybersecurity risk appetite and tolerance thresholds, leading to inconsistent risk acceptance decisions and misaligned security investments.
existingControls: Enterprise risk management framework exists at corporate level
Informal risk acceptance by department heads
Risk register maintained with basic impact ratings
risks.treatmentPlan: Define formal cybersecurity risk appetite statement with quantified thresholds per impact dimension; obtain board approval; cascade risk tolerance levels to operational teams; integrate risk appetite into investment prioritization and risk acceptance processes.

risks.title: Supply Chain Risk Blind Spots
risks.description: Organization lacks visibility into cybersecurity risks introduced through its technology supply chain, including software components, cloud services, and hardware vendors.
existingControls: Vendor security questionnaire for new critical vendors
Contractual security requirements in standard terms
Annual review of tier-1 vendor certifications
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement tiered vendor risk assessment program; deploy continuous third-party risk monitoring service; require software bill of materials (SBOM) from critical software vendors; establish supply chain incident notification requirements in all contracts.

risks.title: Third-Party Access Unmonitored
risks.description: Vendor and contractor access to organizational systems is not adequately controlled, monitored, or revoked upon engagement completion, creating persistent unauthorized access paths.
existingControls: VPN-based remote access for vendors
Dedicated vendor accounts in Active Directory
Manual access revocation process upon contract end
risks.treatmentPlan: Eliminate persistent third-party network access entirely; migrate all vendor interactions to time-boxed, broker-mediated access sessions via zero-trust network access (ZTNA) gateway; require vendors to operate within isolated sandbox environments with no lateral access; replace VPN-based vendor connectivity with audited API integrations where feasible.

risks.title: Cybersecurity Not Integrated into ERM
risks.description: Cybersecurity risks are managed in isolation from the enterprise risk management program, leading to fragmented risk visibility, misaligned priorities, and insufficient executive oversight.
existingControls: Cyber risks mentioned in enterprise risk register
CISO attends enterprise risk committee meetings
Annual cybersecurity risk briefing to board
risks.treatmentPlan: Accept the residual risk given existing controls (CISO participation in ERM committee, annual board briefing, cyber risks in enterprise register). Current residual risk score of 6 (Medium) falls within organizational risk appetite. Formal acceptance documented by CRO with annual review condition; will re-evaluate if organizational risk appetite changes or a material cyber incident exposes integration gaps.

risks.title: Cybersecurity Workforce Skills Gap
risks.description: Organization lacks sufficient cybersecurity professionals with required skills, creating over-reliance on key individuals, slow incident response, and inability to maintain security operations at required maturity.
existingControls: Core security team of 5 FTEs
Annual professional development budget per team member
MSSP contract for 24/7 monitoring coverage
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct skills gap analysis against NICE Framework competencies; develop 3-year cybersecurity workforce plan; establish rotation program across security domains; invest in security automation to reduce manual workload; create cybersecurity apprenticeship pipeline from internal IT staff.
